title: Lu (surname 盧)

infobox.value: City of Lu, [State of Qi](/wiki/State_of_Qi "State of Qi")

is the [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin") romanization of the [Chinese surname](/wiki/Chinese_surname "Chinese surname") written [盧](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9B%A7 "wikt:盧") in [traditional character](/wiki/Traditional_character "Traditional character") and [卢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%A2 "wikt:卢") in [simplified character](/wiki/Simplified_character "Simplified character"). It is also spelled **Lo** or **Loh** according to the [Cantonese](/wiki/Cantonese "Cantonese") pronunciation, and often spelt **Law** in Singapore. Lu 盧 is the [52nd most common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames "List of common Chinese surnames") in China,[[1]](#cite_note-xinhua-1) shared by 5.6 million people, or 0.475% of the Chinese population as of 2002.[[2]](#cite_note-yuan-2) It is especially common in [Guangdong](/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong"), [Guangxi](/wiki/Guangxi "Guangxi"), [Hainan](/wiki/Hainan "Hainan"), and [Hebei](/wiki/Hebei "Hebei") provinces.[[2]](#cite_note-yuan-2) Lu 盧 is listed 167th in the [Song dynasty](/wiki/Song_dynasty "Song dynasty") [classic text](/wiki/Chinese_classics "Chinese classics") *[Hundred Family Surnames](/wiki/Hundred_Family_Surnames "Hundred Family Surnames")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chinese surname with character 卢/盧 (pinyin: Lú)

For the county 卢氏县, see [Lushi County](/wiki/Lushi_County "Lushi County"). For other Chinese surnames also romanized as "Lu", see [Lu (surname)](/wiki/Lu_(surname) "Lu (surname)").

Lú (盧/卢)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Pronunciation | Lú (Mandarin) Lo (Cantonese) |
| Language | [Chinese](/wiki/Chinese_language "Chinese language") |
| Origin | |
| Language | [Old Chinese](/wiki/Old_Chinese "Old Chinese") |
| Word/name | City of Lu, [State of Qi](/wiki/State_of_Qi "State of Qi") |
| Other names | |
| Derivatives | Lư/Lô (Vietnamese) Roh/Noh/Ro/No (Korean) |

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)

Look up ***[卢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%A2 "wiktionary:卢")*** in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.

**Lú** is the [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin") romanization of the [Chinese surname](/wiki/Chinese_surname "Chinese surname") written [盧](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E7%9B%A7 "wikt:盧") in [traditional character](/wiki/Traditional_character "Traditional character") and [卢](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E5%8D%A2 "wikt:卢") in [simplified character](/wiki/Simplified_character "Simplified character"). It is also spelled **Lo** or **Loh** according to the [Cantonese](/wiki/Cantonese "Cantonese") pronunciation, and often spelt **Law** in Singapore. Lu 盧 is the [52nd most common surname](/wiki/List_of_common_Chinese_surnames "List of common Chinese surnames") in China,[[1]](#cite_note-xinhua-1) shared by 5.6 million people, or 0.475% of the Chinese population as of 2002.[[2]](#cite_note-yuan-2) It is especially common in [Guangdong](/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong"), [Guangxi](/wiki/Guangxi "Guangxi"), [Hainan](/wiki/Hainan "Hainan"), and [Hebei](/wiki/Hebei "Hebei") provinces.[[2]](#cite_note-yuan-2) Lu 盧 is listed 167th in the [Song dynasty](/wiki/Song_dynasty "Song dynasty") [classic text](/wiki/Chinese_classics "Chinese classics") *[Hundred Family Surnames](/wiki/Hundred_Family_Surnames "Hundred Family Surnames")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2019 it was the 50th most common surname in mainland China.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lu_(surname_%E7%9B%A7)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Origins")]

According to the [Tang dynasty](/wiki/Tang_dynasty "Tang dynasty") genealogy text *[Yuanhe Xing Zuan](/wiki/Yuanhe_Xing_Zuan "Yuanhe Xing Zuan")*, the surname Lu 盧 originated in the [State of Qi](/wiki/State_of_Qi "State of Qi") during the [Spring and Autumn period](/wiki/Spring_and_Autumn_period "Spring and Autumn period"), and descended from [Gao Xi](/w/index.php?title=Gao_Xi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gao Xi (page does not exist)") (高傒). Gao Xi was the grandson of Prince Gao, who was a son of [Duke Wen of Qi](/wiki/Duke_Wen_of_Qi "Duke Wen of Qi") (reigned 815–804 BC) and a descendant of [Lü Shang](/wiki/Jiang_Ziya "Jiang Ziya"), the founder of Qi. When the Qi ruler [Wuzhi](/wiki/Wuzhi_(Qi) "Wuzhi (Qi)") was murdered in 685 BC, Gao Xi, then prime minister of Qi, helped to install Prince Xiaobai on the throne, to be known as [Duke Huan of Qi](/wiki/Duke_Huan_of_Qi "Duke Huan of Qi"), one of the [Five Hegemons](/wiki/Five_Hegemons "Five Hegemons") of the Spring and Autumn period.[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5)[[6]](#cite_note-hnfo-6)[[7]](#cite_note-shangdu-7) In gratitude, Duke Huan enfeoffed Gao Xi at the city of Lu 盧 (in modern [Changqing District](/wiki/Changqing_District "Changqing District"), [Shandong](/wiki/Shandong "Shandong") province), and many of Gao's descendants adopted Lu 盧 as their surname. This is the main origin of the surname, and Gao Xi is regarded as the founding ancestor of the Lu 盧 surname.[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5)[[7]](#cite_note-shangdu-7)

According to the [Song dynasty](/wiki/Song_dynasty "Song dynasty") encyclopedia *[Tongzhi](/wiki/Tongzhi_(encyclopedia) "Tongzhi (encyclopedia)")*, there was a second source of the Lu 盧 surname from the State of Qi. A branch of Duke Huan of Qi's descendants had the surname Lupu (盧蒲), which was later shortened to Lu.[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5)[[6]](#cite_note-hnfo-6)

A separate source of Lu was the minor state of Luzi 廬子 or Lu 廬 (in modern [Anhui](/wiki/Anhui "Anhui") province) during the Spring and Autumn period. The descendants of Jili (戢黎), a nobleman of Lu, adopted the name of their state as their surname. Lu 廬 was later simplified to Lu 盧.[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5)[[6]](#cite_note-hnfo-6)

## Distribution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lu_(surname_%E7%9B%A7)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Distribution")]

Of the top 30 cities in China, 卢 ranked 9th most common in the city of [Nanning](/wiki/Nanning "Nanning").[[8]](#cite_note-ranking-8)

## Later adoption

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lu_(surname_%E7%9B%A7)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Later adoption")]

During the [Xianbei](/wiki/Xianbei "Xianbei") [Northern Wei](/wiki/Northern_Wei "Northern Wei") dynasty, [Emperor Xiaowen](/wiki/Emperor_Xiaowen_of_Northern_Wei "Emperor Xiaowen of Northern Wei") (reigned 467–499 AD) implemented a drastic policy of [sinicization](/wiki/Sinicization "Sinicization"), ordering his own people to [adopt Chinese surnames](/wiki/Change_of_Xianbei_names_to_Han_names "Change of Xianbei names to Han names"). The Tufulu (吐伏盧) and Molu (莫盧) clans of Xianbei adopted Lu as their surname. The Xianbei people have since completely assimilated into the [Han Chinese](/wiki/Han_Chinese "Han Chinese").[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5)[[6]](#cite_note-hnfo-6)

According to the *[Book of Sui](/wiki/Book_of_Sui "Book of Sui")*, Zhangchou Taiyi (章仇太翼), a native of [Hejian Commandery](/wiki/Hejian_Commandery "Hejian Commandery") (in modern [Cangzhou](/wiki/Cangzhou "Cangzhou"), [Hebei](/wiki/Hebei "Hebei")) was a famous scholar of the time. [Emperor Yang of Sui](/wiki/Emperor_Yang_of_Sui "Emperor Yang of Sui") granted him the surname Lu 盧. Zhangchou subsequently became known as Lu Taiyi, and was the ancestor of the prosperous Hejian Lu clan.[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5)

## Lu clan of Fanyang

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lu_(surname_%E7%9B%A7)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lu clan of Fanyang")]

Main article: [Lu clan of Fanyang](/wiki/Lu_clan_of_Fanyang "Lu clan of Fanyang")

In the fourth century BC, the throne of the Qi state was usurped by the Tian clan. Many aristocratic clans that descended from the old ruling house of Jiang (姜), including Lu, fled the state and dispersed all over China.[[5]](#cite_note-henan-5) During the [Qin dynasty](/wiki/Qin_dynasty "Qin dynasty"), the erudite [Lu Ao](/w/index.php?title=Lu_Ao&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lu Ao (page does not exist)") (盧敖) settled in [Fanyang Commandery](/wiki/Fanyang_Commandery "Fanyang Commandery") (modern Beijing). The Fanyang Lu clan later became exceedingly prosperous. During the [Jin dynasty (266–420)](/wiki/Jin_dynasty_(266%E2%80%93420) "Jin dynasty (266–420)"), the Fanyang Lu, together with the [Cui clan of Boling](/wiki/Cui_clan_of_Boling "Cui clan of Boling"), the [Wang clan of Taiyuan](/wiki/Wang_clan_of_Taiyuan "Wang clan of Taiyuan"), the [Zheng clan of Xingyang](/wiki/Zheng_clan_of_Xingyang "Zheng clan of Xingyang"), and the [Li clan of Zhao](/w/index.php?title=Li_clan_of_Zhao&action=edit&redlink=1 "Li clan of Zhao (page does not exist)"), were considered the five most prominent clans in China (海內五大望族).[[7]](#cite_note-shangdu-7) During the [Tang dynasty](/wiki/Tang_dynasty "Tang dynasty"), eight [chancellors](/wiki/Chancellor_of_the_Tang_dynasty "Chancellor of the Tang dynasty") were surnamed Lu 盧, including several from Fanyang.[[6]](#cite_note-hnfo-6)

During the Tang dynasty the Li clan of Zhao [趙郡李氏](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E8%B6%99%E9%83%A1%E6%9D%8E%E6%B0%8F "zh:趙郡李氏"), the Cui clan of Boling, the [Cui clan of Qinghe](/wiki/Cui_clan_of_Qinghe "Cui clan of Qinghe"), the Lu clan of Fanyang, the Zheng clan of Xingyang [滎陽鄭氏](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%BB%8E%E9%99%BD%E9%84%AD%E6%B0%8F "zh:滎陽鄭氏"), the Wang clan of Taiyuan [太原王氏](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%A4%AA%E5%8E%9F%E7%8E%8B%E6%B0%8F "zh:太原王氏"), and the Li clan of Longxi [隴西李氏](https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E9%9A%B4%E8%A5%BF%E6%9D%8E%E6%B0%8F "zh:隴西李氏") were the seven noble clans between whom marriage was banned by law.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Their status as "Seven Great surnames" became known during Gaozong's rule.[[10]](#cite_note-Nienhauser2010-10)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lu_(surname_%E7%9B%A7)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Lu Wan](/wiki/Lu_Wan "Lu Wan") (盧綰; 256–194 BC), [Western Han](/wiki/Western_Han "Western Han") dynasty general
* [Lu Zhi](/wiki/Lu_Zhi_(Han_dynasty) "Lu Zhi (Han dynasty)") (盧植; died 192 AD), [Eastern Han](/wiki/Eastern_Han "Eastern Han") dynasty politician
* [Lu Sidao](/wiki/Lu_Sidao "Lu Sidao") (盧思道; 531–582), [Sui dynasty](/wiki/Sui_dynasty "Sui dynasty") poet
* [Lu Chengqing](/wiki/Lu_Chengqing "Lu Chengqing") (盧承慶; 595–670), [Tang dynasty chancellor](/wiki/Tang_dynasty_chancellor "Tang dynasty chancellor")
* [Lu Zhaolin](/wiki/Lu_Zhaolin "Lu Zhaolin") (盧照鄰; ca. 634 – ca. 686) Tang dynasty poet
* [Lu Huaishen](/wiki/Lu_Huaishen "Lu Huaishen") (盧懷慎; died 716), Tang dynasty chancellor
* [Lu Qi](/wiki/Lu_Qi_(Tang_dynasty) "Lu Qi (Tang dynasty)") (盧杞; died 785), Tang dynasty chancellor
* [Lu Lun](/wiki/Lu_Lun "Lu Lun") (盧綸; 739–799), [Tang dynasty](/wiki/Tang_dynasty "Tang dynasty") poet
* [Lu Han](/wiki/Lu_Han_(Tang_dynasty) "Lu Han (Tang dynasty)") (盧翰; fl. 8th century), Tang dynasty chancellor
* [Lu Tong](/wiki/Lu_Tong "Lu Tong") (盧仝; died 835), Tang dynasty poet
* [Lu Shang](/wiki/Lu_Shang "Lu Shang") (盧商; 789–859), Tang dynasty chancellor
* [Lu Xi](/wiki/Lu_Xi "Lu Xi") (盧攜; died 881), Tang dynasty chancellor
* [Lu Zhi](/wiki/Lu_Zhi_(poet) "Lu Zhi (poet)") (盧摯; 1243–1315), [Yuan dynasty](/wiki/Yuan_dynasty "Yuan dynasty") poet
* [Lu Xiangsheng](/w/index.php?title=Lu_Xiangsheng&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lu Xiangsheng (page does not exist)") (盧象昇; 1600–1639), Ming dynasty general of Tianxiong Army
* [Lu Kun](/wiki/Lu_Kun "Lu Kun") (盧坤; 1772–1835), [Qing dynasty](/wiki/Qing_dynasty "Qing dynasty") viceroy of [Huguang](/wiki/Huguang "Huguang") and [Liangguang](/wiki/Liangguang "Liangguang")
* [Lu Muzhen](/wiki/Lu_Muzhen "Lu Muzhen") (盧慕貞; 1867–1952), [Sun Yat-sen](/wiki/Sun_Yat-sen "Sun Yat-sen")'s first wife
* [Lu Yongxiang](/wiki/Lu_Yongxiang_(warlord) "Lu Yongxiang (warlord)") (盧永祥; 1867–1933), warlord
* [C. T. Loo](/wiki/C._T._Loo "C. T. Loo") (盧芹齋, Lu Qinzhai; 1880–1957), art dealer
* [Lu Han](/wiki/Lu_Han_(general) "Lu Han (general)") (盧漢; 1895–1974), [Kuomintang](/wiki/Kuomintang "Kuomintang") general
* [Lu Jiaxi (chemist)](/wiki/Lu_Jiaxi_(chemist) "Lu Jiaxi (chemist)") (卢嘉锡; 1915–2001), chemist, President of the [Chinese Academy of Sciences](/wiki/Chinese_Academy_of_Sciences "Chinese Academy of Sciences")
* [Lisa Lu](/wiki/Lisa_Lu "Lisa Lu") (盧燕; Lu Yan; born 1927), actress
* [Lu Yen](/wiki/Lu_Yen "Lu Yen") (盧炎; Lu Yan; 1930–2008), Taiwanese composer
* [Lu Yonggen](/wiki/Lu_Yonggen "Lu Yonggen") ( 卢永根; 1930–2019), plant geneticist, President of [South China Agricultural University](/wiki/South_China_Agricultural_University "South China Agricultural University")
* [Lu Ruihua](/wiki/Lu_Ruihua "Lu Ruihua") (卢瑞华; 1938–2025), Governor of [Guangdong](/wiki/Guangdong "Guangdong") province
* [Lo Hoi-pang](/wiki/Lo_Hoi-pang "Lo Hoi-pang") (盧海鵬; Lu Haipeng; born 1941), Hong Kong actor
* [Lu Hsiu-yi](/wiki/Lu_Hsiu-yi "Lu Hsiu-yi") (盧修一; Liu Xiuyi; 1941–1998), Taiwanese politician
* [Lowell Lo](/wiki/Lowell_Lo "Lowell Lo") (盧冠廷; Lu Guanting; born 1950), Hong Kong musician
* [Anna Lo](/wiki/Anna_Lo "Anna Lo") (盧曼華; Lo Manwah; born 1950), Hong Kong-born Northern Ireland politician
* [Lu Zhangong](/wiki/Lu_Zhangong "Lu Zhangong") (卢展工; born 1952), party chief of [Fujian](/wiki/Fujian "Fujian") and [Henan](/wiki/Henan "Henan") provinces
* [Ken Lo](/wiki/Ken_Lo "Ken Lo") (盧惠光; Lu Huiguang; born 1959), Hong Kong actor and martial artist
* [Money Lo](/wiki/Money_Lo "Money Lo") (盧敏儀, born 1960), a Hong Kong actress
* [Lu Shiow-yen](/wiki/Lu_Shiow-yen "Lu Shiow-yen") (盧秀燕, born 1961), a Taiwanese politician and former television presenter

* [Lu Shaye](/wiki/Lu_Shaye "Lu Shaye") (卢沙野; born 1964), diplomat
* [Calvin Lo](/wiki/Calvin_Lo_(businessman) "Calvin Lo (businessman)") (盧啟賢), Hong Kong businessman

* [Lu Rui En](/wiki/Rui_En "Rui En") (卢瑞恩; born 1981), Singaporean actress and singer
* [Lu Yen-Hsun](/wiki/Lu_Yen-Hsun "Lu Yen-Hsun") (盧彥勳; Lu Yanxun; born 1983), Taiwanese tennis player
* [Lo Kwan Yee](/wiki/Lo_Kwan_Yee "Lo Kwan Yee") (盧均宜; Lu Junyi; born 1984), Hong Kong football player
* [Lo Chun Kit](/wiki/Lo_Chun_Kit "Lo Chun Kit") (盧俊傑; Lu Junjie; born 1985), Hong Kong football player
* [Lu Ying-chi](/wiki/Lu_Ying-chi "Lu Ying-chi") (盧映錡; born 1985), Taiwanese weightlifter
* [Crowd Lu](/wiki/Crowd_Lu "Crowd Lu") (盧廣仲; Lu Guangzhong; born 1985), Taiwanese singer-songwriter
* [Lu Lin](/wiki/Lu_Lin_(footballer) "Lu Lin (footballer)") (卢琳; born 1985), football player
* [Ellen Joyce Loo](/wiki/Ellen_Joyce_Loo "Ellen Joyce Loo") (盧凱彤; Lu Kaitong; 1986–2018), Hong Kong–based Canadian singer-songwriter
* [Lu Lan](/wiki/Lu_Lan "Lu Lan") (卢兰; born 1987), badminton player
* [Lu Junyi](/wiki/Lu_Junyi "Lu Junyi") (盧俊義), fictional character in the novel *[Water Margin](/wiki/Water_Margin "Water Margin")*
* [Anson Lo](/wiki/Anson_Lo "Anson Lo") (盧瀚霆; born 1995), Hong Kong singer, dancer and actor
* [Lu Yuxiao](/wiki/Lu_Yuxiao "Lu Yuxiao") (盧昱曉; born 1999), Chinese actress

## Notable Korean people with the 盧 surname

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lu_(surname_%E7%9B%A7)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notable Korean people with the 盧 surname")]

Further information: [Roh (name)](/wiki/Roh_(name) "Roh (name)")

* [Lho Shin-yong](/wiki/Lho_Shin-yong "Lho Shin-yong") (盧信永; 1930–2019), 18th Prime Minister of South Korea
* [Roh Tae-woo](/wiki/Roh_Tae-woo "Roh Tae-woo") (盧泰愚; 1932–2021), former South Korean president
* [Ro Jai-bong](/wiki/Ro_Jai-bong "Ro Jai-bong") (盧在鳳; 1936–2024), 22nd Prime Minister of South Korea
* [Roh Moo-hyun](/wiki/Roh_Moo-hyun "Roh Moo-hyun") (盧武鉉; 1946–2009), former South Korean president
